title: rhus virens var. virens
intro: Rhus virens var. virens, also known as the poison sumac, is a plant species native to the southeastern United States and is characterized by its glossy leaves and distinctive white or greenish berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of tannins and gallic acid. The health benefits of Rhus virens var. virens include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its use in reducing fever and relieving pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to treat fever and other ailments.
